svg.text: select demodb.employees.id AS id,demodb.employees.name AS name,demodb.employees.manager_id AS manager_id,1 AS level
select e.id AS id,e.name AS name,e.manager_id AS manager_id,(oc.level + 1) AS oc.level + 1
select org_chart.level AS level,count(0) AS headcount
Bar width = self-time (time in this operation only). Darker bars = slower.
Where a warning applies: hover the bar — it will show “In Query Analysis” and the info panel lists the bar label.
index_condition_pushdown=on — Parts of the WHERE clause are evaluated inside the storage engine using index columns before fetching the full row — reduces rows handed to the server
materialization=on — A subquery or derived table is executed once and its result stored in a temporary table for reuse. Watch tmp_table_size / max_heap_table_size — it may spill to
Full table scan: <temporary> (10 rows), org_chart (1000 rows)
1 temp table(s) (Materialize) — up to 1000 rows; may spill to disk
1 sort operation(s) — 10 rows; may use disk-based filesort
• Add indexes on filter/join columns to avoid full table scans
• Increase tmp_table_size / max_heap_table_size to keep temp tables in memory
• Increase sort_buffer_size or add an ordered index to avoid filesort
